channel.title: Posts on Nikhil Vemula
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Nikhil Vemula

item.title: Ruby
item.description: Ruby is also great for serving web pages, generating dynamic web page content and excels at database access tasks.

item.title: CPP STL
item.description: Vector Note: g++ -st=cpp14 filename.cpp #include <iostream>#include <vector> using namespace std; void printVector(vector<int> vect); bool f(int x, int y) { return x > y; } int main() { vector<int> vect = { 11, 3, 2, 14 }; printVector(vect); sort(vect.begin(), vect.end()); // Sort using nlogn printVector(vect); bool present = binary_search(vect.begin(), vect.end(), 3); cout << present <<endl; vect.push_back(100); vect.push_back(100); vect.push_back(100); vect.push_back(123); printVector(vect); // lower_bound -> give me iterator >= 100 vector<int>::iterator itr = lower_bound(vect.
